nodes.label: AI as research starting point and fact-checker
nodes.label_negative: AI as sole source of knowledge without personal understanding
nodes.grounding_span: I'm basically using it as a starting point for my own research, and then I return to it to fact-check my research

nodes.label: AI for novel unfamiliar tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I only really turn to AI when something new turns up

nodes.label: AI for simple how-to questions
nodes.grounding_span: It's useful when I ask simple 'how to' questions though

nodes.label: AI as delegation tool rather than collaborator
nodes.label_negative: AI as collaborative partner throughout process
nodes.grounding_span: It's much more delegation than it is collaboration

nodes.label: Maintaining personal knowledge and skills
nodes.grounding_span: I'm very keen to maintain my knowledge and I guess I see over-reliance on AI as a way to diminish what I know

nodes.label: Career adaptability and future-proofing
nodes.grounding_span: the only option is to widen that skillset now while AI is still developing

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for research and verification
nodes.grounding_span: I pretty much use it exclusively for research

nodes.label: Frustration with AI for nuanced bookkeeping
Proactive concern about job displacement
nodes.grounding_span: I'm definitely aware that AI could cover that scope in the near or distant future

nodes.label: Intentional limitation of AI use to preserve knowledge
nodes.grounding_span: I don't think I'd widen the way I use it any time soon

nodes.label: Step-by-step decomposition of tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I just find it simpler to break it down at every step

nodes.label: Learning-by-doing with AI as check
nodes.grounding_span: I'll use this question as a starting point for my own study, rather than asking the AI for the answer
